title: How to File a Consumer Complaint
description: Step-by-step guide to filing a complaint for defective goods or deficient services

steps.action: First, try to resolve with the seller/company directly
steps.details: Send a written complaint (email/letter) to the company's customer care or grievance officer. Keep a copy and note the complaint reference number. Give them 15-30 days to respond.
If the company doesn't respond or refuses to help, send a formal legal notice through a lawyer or by registered post. This is not mandatory but often prompts action.

steps.action: File complaint on National Consumer Helpline
steps.details: Call 1800-11-4000 (toll-free) or file online at consumerhelpline.gov.in. They will mediate between you and the company. This is a free and fast alternative before going to consumer court.

steps.action: File in Consumer Court (if mediation fails)
steps.details: File your complaint online at edaakhil.nic.in or in person at: District Commission (up to ₹1 crore), State Commission (₹1 crore - ₹10 crore), National Commission (above ₹10 crore).

Include: Your name and address, Opposite party details (seller/company), Facts of the case chronologically, Defect/deficiency details, Relief sought (refund/replacement/compensation), List of documents attached.
No court fee for the first ₹5 lakh. Nominal fee for higher amounts. Senior citizens and SC/ST get fee concessions.
You can represent yourself (no lawyer needed). Attend hearings on scheduled dates. Present your evidence and arguments. The court must decide within 3-5 months.

required_documents: Written complaint to the company and their response
tips: You DON'T need a lawyer — you can argue your own case
No court fee for complaints up to ₹5 lakh
File complaints ONLINE at edaakhil.nic.in from anywhere
E-commerce purchases have the same protections
You can claim compensation for mental agony too
Time limit: File within 2 years of the cause of action
